folio.label: 165r
folio.l: haue it of hi~ self ,

And after æat , æè shalt considre æine frendes , &
thyne enemys ,

And as touchyng thy frendes , æè shalt considre
whicú of hem ben most feitúful , & most wyse , & eldest & most
appreued in cou~seillyngö ,

And of he~ shalt æè axe thy cou~seil ,
as the cas requiretú

¢ I seye , æ first ©e shuln clepe to ©oure
cou~seil , ©oure frendes that ben trewe , 189Cross in dry point in the left margin.ODR

For Salamon seitú , æ
for rigút as the herte of a man , delitetú in sauour æ is swo-
te , rigút so the cou~seil of trewe frendes , ©euetú swetnesse to
the soule ,,

he seitú also , there may no thyng , be likned to æe
trewe frend ,

for certes , gold ne siluä ben nat so meche wortú
as the good wil , of a trewe frend , ,

And eke he seitú , that a tr-
ewe frend , is a st¼ng defence , who so æ it fyndetú , certes he fyn-
detú a gret tresor

¢ Tha~ne shuln ©e eke considre , if æ ©ourö tre-
we frendes , ben discrete & wyse , for the book seitú , Axe alwey
thy cou~seil of hem , æ ben wyse ,

& by this same reson , shuln ©e
clepen to ©oure cou~seil of ©oure frendes æ ben of age , swicú as han
seyn , & ben expert in manye thynges , & ben appreued i~ cou~seil-
lynges

¢ For the book seith , in olde men is al the sapience , & i~
longe tyme the pèdence

¢ And Tullyus seitú , æ grete æinges
ne ben ay acomplissed by strengthe , ne be delyuernesse of body ,
but by good cou~seil , by auctorite of ¹sones & by Science ,, The
whicú thre thynges , ne ben nat fieble by age , but cätes æei en-
forcen , & encresen day by day ,

& tha~ne shuln ©e kepe this , for
a genäal reule , , First ©e shuln clepe to ©oure cou~seil , a fewe of
©oure frendes æ ben especiale

¢ For Salamon seitú , many fren-
des haue æè , but a mong a thousand , chese the oon , to be thy
cou~seilloå ,

for al it so be , æ æè first ne telle thy cou~seil , but to
a fewe , æè maist afterward , telle it to moo folk , if it be nede ,

but
loke alwey , æ æine cou~seilloås haue thilke thre condiciou~s , æ
I haue seid bifore , æ is to seye ; æ they be trewe , wyse , & olde
ex¹ience ,

and werk nat alwey in euäy nede , by oo cou~seilloå allone ,
for somtyme bihouetú it to be cou~seiled by manye , ,,

For Sala-
mon seitú , Saluaciou~ of thynges , is where as æäe ben many
cou~seilloås

¢ Now sithe æ I haue told ©ow , of whicú folk , ©e shul-
de be cou~seilled , now wol I teche ©ow , whicú cou~seil ©e ougúte
eschue

¢ First ©e shuln eschue , æe cou~seil of fooles ¢ For Sala-
mon seitú , take no cou~seil of a fool , for he ne can nat cou~-
seille , but after his owen lust , & his affecco~n ,

the book seitú , the
¸prete of a fool , is this ; he trowetú ligútly harm , of euäy wigút
& ligútly trowetú al bounte , in hi~ self ,

¢ Thow shalt eke eschue
the cou~seillyng of alle flatereres , swicú as enforcen he rathere
to preysen ©oure ¹sone , by flat äye , than for to telle ©ow , the soth-
fastnesse of thynges ,
wherfore Tullyus seitú , A mong alle æe
